title: Heat Exchanger Effectiveness — NTU Method
content: The Number of Transfer Units (NTU) method characterises heat exchanger performance: ε = Q_actual/Q_max, where Q_max = C_min·(T_h,in − T_c,in). For a balanced counter-flow exchanger (C_r = 1): ε = NTU/(1+NTU). In general: NTU = −ln((1−ε(1+C_r))/(1+C_r)). Effectiveness ε and NTU are determined experimentally from four temperature measurements (hot/cold inlet/outlet) and flow rates.
